commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And she said, Let your handmaid find grace in your sight. So the woman went her way, and did eat, and her countenance was no more sad. Eyes: that thou wouldst lay aside thy suspicions against my character, and pray (Calmet) that I may obtain so great a happiness. Hebrew, "no more "as it had been sad, and defaced with tears. (Haydock) She was now full of hope and joy. (Menochius) Septuagint, "her countenance fell not. "She was not moved with anger or with jealousy, Genesis iv.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
